title: HAProxy SSL Handshake and Client Certificate Failures
dataSources: `index=proxy` TLS termination logs (`sourcetype=haproxy:tcp` or `haproxy:http` with SSL fields)
app: HAProxy syslog with `%sslc` / `%sslfc` log variables or custom format
spl:
index=proxy (sourcetype="haproxy:http" OR sourcetype="haproxy:tcp")
| search "SSL" ("handshake failure" OR "verify error" OR "alert unknown ca")
| stats count by host
| sort -count
description: TLS failures may indicate expired client certs, weak ciphers, or MITM attempts on public listeners.
value: Preserves cryptographic hygiene for zero-trust and mutual TLS designs.
implementation: Extend `log-format` with SSL variables; sample baseline noise before alerting.
visualization: Timechart (SSL errors), table (frontend), link to PKI calendar.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: HAProxy syslog with `%sslc` / `%sslfc` log variables or custom format.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=proxy` TLS termination logs (`sourcetype=haproxy:tcp` or `haproxy:http` with SSL fields).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Match phrases to your HAProxy TLS log output; many shops ship TLS termination logs only at info level.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy (sourcetype="haproxy:http" OR sourcetype="haproxy:tcp")
| search "SSL" ("handshake failure" OR "verify error" OR "alert unknown ca")
| stats count by host
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**HAProxy SSL Handshake and Client Certificate Failures** ‚Äî See the description and value fields in this use case JSON.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=proxy` TLS termination logs (`sourcetype=haproxy:tcp` or `haproxy:http` with SSL fields). **App/TA**: HAProxy syslog with `%sslc` / `%sslfc` log variables or custom format. Rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` to match your deployment.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scope to the documented index and sourcetype, then apply transforms, thresholds, and `timechart`/`stats` as in the SPL above.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm events exist, field extractions match your format, and thresholds reflect production baselines.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add to a dashboard or alert; document ownership. Suggested visuals: Timechart (SSL errors), table (frontend), link to PKI calendar..
grandmaExplanation: We track bad handshakes on your load balancer so certificate and cipher issues do not linger.
